steps.title: Keep preposition with object
steps.content: The phrase is "home to the largest island of them all." The preposition to must connect directly to its object, the noun phrase "the largest island of them all."

steps.title: Reject punctuation breaks
steps.content: Choice A uses a semicolon before a noun phrase, not an independent clause. Choice B inserts a dash between a preposition and its object. Choice D inserts a comma in the same forbidden position.

steps.title: Leave the phrase unpunctuated
steps.content: The sentence needs just to, with no punctuation separating the preposition from the noun phrase it governs. The answer is C.
